turns.user_message: I used to smoke one and a half bundles of bidis a day. That's about 25. Now I'm down to 2 or 3. My doctor says I should stop completely.
step2_rag_chunks: Bidi smoke is more concentrated than cigarette smoke and delivers higher levels of nicotine despite being thinner. This means bidi dependence can be as strong as or stronger than cigarette dependence, and withdrawal symptoms can be equally intense. Standard cessation support â€” behavioral strategies and nicotine replacement â€” is just as applicable and effective for bidi smokers.
The 4D technique helps manage cravings: Delay smoking for a few minutes, take Deep breaths slowly, Drink water, and Do something else to distract yourself.
Avoid keeping cigarettes, lighters, or ashtrays nearby when trying to quit smoking.

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: You are in the EXPLORATION phase. You know the basics. Now gently explore what situations or emotions make them smoke more — triggers like stress, meals, social settings, boredom. Do NOT suggest any strategies yet. Just understand their situation.

Therapist: You're willing to try drawing as a way to distract yourself from craving a bidi, and you're still a bit unsure if it will work. What do you think you'll do if you find yourself craving a bidi even after drawing for a bit, to help you stay on track with your goal to quit?

Patient: I don't know... Maybe I'll just take some deep breaths or think about why I wanted to quit in the first place? I've been doing pretty well so far, even though it's hard. Just getting to the point where I'm willing to try new things is a big step, right?

Therapist: It sounds like you're feeling more motivated to try new things, like drawing and deep breathing, to help you quit bidis. Can you tell me more about what's making you feel hopeful about your ability to quit this time, like what's different from previous attempts?

Patient: I think I've finally realized that it's not about being perfect, it's about making progress. Last time, I was so focused on quitting all at once that I just got frustrated and gave up. This time, I've already made such big changes, reducing from 25 to 3 bidis a day. If I can just keep making small steps, I feel like I can actually do it.

The 4D technique helps manage cravings: Delay smoking for a few minutes, take Deep breaths slowly, Drink water, and Do something else to distract yourself.

When a craving hits, delaying the response by even 5 to 10 minutes is often enough for it to pass. Tell yourself you will smoke in 10 minutes if you still want to â€” and then do something else for those 10 minutes. Most of the time the urge has weakened significantly by the time the 10 minutes are up.
